message:
refactor(storage): replace rusqlite FTS lifecycle with native frankensqlite rebuild

The FTS schema was being maintained through a two-phase process: open with
frankensqlite for migrations, close, reopen with rusqlite for FTS consistency
checks, then reopen with frankensqlite again. This added complexity, extra
connection churn, and potential for the two SQLite drivers to disagree on
schema state.

Replace the entire rusqlite-based FTS lifecycle in FrankenStorage::open with
a single rebuild_fts_via_frankensqlite() call that drops and recreates the
FTS table using the already-open frankensqlite connection. This eliminates
the close-reopen-close-reopen cycle and keeps the FTS schema under a single
driver's authority.

- Add rebuild_fts_via_frankensqlite() which drops fts_messages, recreates
  it via FTS5_REGISTER_SQL, and populates from messages/conversations/agents
  tables in a single pass
- Simplify FrankenStorage::open to call rebuild_fts_via_frankensqlite()
  directly after migrations, removing the intermediate rusqlite verification
- Update rebuild_fts() public API to delegate to the new method
- Simplify reset_storage test helper: remove db_path parameter, use
  storage.rebuild_fts() instead of manual drop + rusqlite materialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
